A 160-g billiard ball moving to the right at 2.75 m/s collides with another 160-g billiard ball that is at rest. The two balls move off as shown above after the collision. Find the speed of the ball that moved off at 60$^{\circ}$.

A 1.66-kg solid ball with a radius of 18.2 cm is thrown along a level surface so that at $t = 0$, it slides with a linear speed of 2.44 m/s, but does not rotate. As it slides, it begins to spin and eventually rolls without slipping. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the ball and the surface is 0.122. The ball begins to roll without slipping at $t =$ __________ sec.

Nickolas was standing still at an intersection when a police car came down the street at a high speed. The frequency of the police car siren as the car approached was 1676 Hz. The frequency after it passed was 1358 Hz. How fast was the police car traveling? The air temperature was 20$^{\circ}$C.

A raft is made using 12 logs that are lashed together. Each log is 7.20 m in length and has a diameter of 42.0 cm. How many 75.0-kg men can stand on the raft without getting their feet wet? The specific gravity of the logs is 0.622 and the density of water is 1000 kg/m$^3$.
An RLC circuit consists of $R = 120 \, \Omega$, $L = 60.0$ mH, and $C = 9.00 \, \mu\text{F}$. The power comes from a 120-V ac (rms) 600 Hz source. Find the rms voltage across the inductor.

A charge $Q_1 = +600$ nC is distributed uniformly throughout a nonconducting sphere which has a radius of 0.624 m. What is the magnitude of the electric field inside the sphere, 0.312 m from the center?

An electron traveling horizontally at $1.15 \times 10^7$ m/s enters a region which has a uniform 2.33-mT magnetic field oriented vertically upward. The electron travels in a circular path with a radius of __________.

A conducting rod rests on two long parallel frictionless rails that are 2.43 cm apart. The rod has a mass of 2.88 g. A uniform 316 mT magnetic field is coming out of the page and is perpendicular to the rod and the rails. At $t = 0$, a source of emf is connected to the points A and B. If the source puts out a constant current of 822 mA, find the speed of the rod at $t = 35.5$ ms.

A 2.00-inch-tall candle is placed 36.0 cm from a converging lens with a focal length of 24.0 cm. A second converging lens with a focal length of 24.0 cm is placed 60.0 cm from the first lens. The final image is __________ cm from the candle.
Light with a wavelength of 440 nm strikes a target and photoelectrons are emitted. The shortest de Broglie wavelength of these photoelectrons is 1.226 nm. Find the work function of the target.

The binding energy per nucleon of the $^{56}_{26}\text{Fe}$ nucleus is __________ MeV/nucleon. Atomic mass of $^{56}_{26}\text{Fe} = 55.934942$ u. Atomic mass of a proton = 1.007825 u. Atomic mass of a neutron = 1.008665 u. 1 u = 931.5 MeV.

The MIT Physics I class performed an experiment in which a 50.0-g bullet is fired at a specified speed and becomes embedded in the edge of a 3.00 kg cylinder. The cylinder is free to rotate about its axis of symmetry. Assume there is no frictional torque. One of the student groups recorded the linear velocity of the bullet and the resulting angular velocity of the bullet/cylinder combination for five trials. They calculated a LSRL for their results. Use the graph above and find the radius of the cylinder.

The physics class at Argyle did an experiment using a diffraction grating and several sources of monochromatic light.
